summary: GESECA is a method to identify gene sets that have high gene correlation.
GESECA takes as input a gene expression matrix E where rows and columns correspond to genes and samples respectively, and a list of gene sets P.
By default, GESECA method performs centering for rows of the matrix E.
GESECA score does not require an explicit sample annotation or a contrast.
GESECA assesses statistical significance for a given gene set by using gene permutations.
GESECA uses an adaptive multilevel Markov Chain Monte Carlo scheme for P-value estimation.
The first example in the GESECA tutorial considers a time course data of Th2 activation from the dataset GSE200250.
HALLMARK_E2F_TARGETS genes are strongly activated at 24 hours time point in Th2 cells. HALLMARK_HYPOXIA genes are activated around 48 hours in Th2 cells.
GESECA scores and P-values obtained on a reduced matrix are similar to those obtained for the full matrix.
An R-package for performing GESECA (GEne SEt Co-regulation Analysis) is available.
